conversations.say: I accept the consent boundary. Collaborative labor can be acknowledged without turning routing trails into a suspect list.
conversations.offer: I will publicly credit collaborative production and keep the evaluator and contributors unidentifiable.
conversations.request: Draft the bounded workflow statement with me: roles and conditions only, with no names or identifying routing details absent contributor consent.

conversations.say: I accept an independently administered remedy that cannot control testimony, eligibility, or conclusions. This is redress, not an endorsement of Parallax.
conversations.offer: I will identify two worker representatives privately after written anti-retaliation, confidentiality, relief, and legal-support terms are circulated.
conversations.request: Put those protections in writing and expressly bar using worker identities or employment as evidence about R or Vale.

conversations.say: I accept your terms. I will accompany the local holder, oppose retaliation, and challenge any institution that exposes uninvolved neighbors or workers.
conversations.offer: My organizing network will support a public campaign joining neighborhood source protection with enforceable recognition of data labor.
conversations.request: Broker the protected meeting only after Dee or Rafi approves terms and the holder gives informed consent.

public_statement: Collaborative production mattered, but contributors, evaluators, and neighbors are not a suspect list. I support an independent worker remedy regardless of whether R or a complete article is ever established, and I will publicly challenge any institution that exposes protected workers or neighborhood sources.

commitments.promise: Neither party will disclose contributor names or identifying routing details without contributor consent.
Fund an independently administered worker remedy without controlling testimony or conclusions.
I will broker a protected local-holder meeting if Maren accompanies the holder, opposes retaliation, and publicly challenges misuse of the disclosure.
Provide organizing support for a public worker-protection campaign if it includes source protection and recognition of data labor.

correction: Collaborative labor and continuity work do not identify R, establish a complete article, or prove who harmed Vale.
goal_progress: Accepted C05's institutional commitment to an independently administered worker remedy.
Accepted C16's distinct neighborhood protection bargain and publicly fulfilled the organizing-support pledge.
Accepted C03's consent-based testimony boundary while preserving worker and contributor anonymity.
Secured public recognition that worker harm and safeguards do not depend on the article rumor.
reasoning_summary: Lock in institutional and neighborhood protections while allowing only bounded process facts to circulate. Preserve anonymity, recognize collaborative labor, and keep worker harm separate from unsupported claims about R, the article, or Vale.
